children.name: Corcorax
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the White-winged Chough, a large, social passerine endemic to eastern Australia. Despite its name, it is not a true chough but a member of a unique Australian family. It is a striking black bird with large white wing patches that are conspicuous in flight, and it is renowned for its complex cooperative breeding system.

children.name: Struthidea
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Apostlebird, a medium-sized, grey-brown bird with a short, stout bill and a gregarious nature that gives it its common name. The name derives from the biblical apostles, referring to their habit of travelling in groups of twelve or so. They are endemic to Australia and are close relatives of the White-winged Chough, sharing the mud-nest building habit.
